page: Amazon Web Services m9gd.8xlarge is a general-purpose instance powered by AWS Graviton processors. Built on the ARMv9 architecture, it features 32 dedicated physical cores using the AWS Neoverse-V3 model running at 2.8 GHz, with one thread per core. The instance is equipped with 128.0 GB of system memory, providing a balanced 4.0 GB of RAM per core. For local storage, it includes a bundled 1900 GB NVMe SSD instance store volume. Network connectivity is supported by a baseline bandwidth of 17 Gbps, managed via the Nitro hypervisor.

Benchmark results show strong multi-core capabilities and memory subsystem efficiency. The instance achieves top-tier status in PassMark memory scores, PassMark database operations, static web serving throughput, and single-threaded gzip compression and bzip3 decompression. Multi-core stress-ng CPU performance, multi-threaded bzip3 decompression, and Redis SET operations fall into the strong category. However, single-core stress-ng CPU performance, uncached memory bandwidth, and LLM inference speeds for both 135M and 7B models remain in the average range.

The ARM64 architecture and dedicated core allocation offer high resource density and predictable compute performance. The inclusion of local NVMe SSD storage reduces reliance on external block storage, enhancing qualitative cost efficiency for data-intensive operations. Given its balanced CPU-to-memory ratio, high database operation benchmarks, and fast local storage, this instance is suited for database hosting, caching layers, web serving, and general-purpose enterprise applications.
description: Amazon Web Services m9gd.8xlarge is a general-purpose instance powered by 32 dedicated ARM64 AWS Graviton (Neoverse-V3) cores running at 2.8 GHz. It features 128.0 GB of memory (4.0 GB per core) and a bundled 1900 GB NVMe SSD instance store volume, operating on the Nitro hypervisor with 17 Gbps baseline network bandwidth. Benchmark data indicates top-tier performance in database operations, static web serving, and PassMark memory scores, alongside strong multi-core CPU and Redis SET performance. Conversely, single-core CPU execution and LLM inference speeds are average. The combination of dedicated ARMv9 cores and high-speed local NVMe storage provides a cost-efficient profile for resource-dense workloads. This instance is suited for database hosting, caching layers, web serving, and general-purpose enterprise applications requiring local scratch space.
og_description: Amazon Web Services m9gd.8xlarge is an ARM64 general-purpose instance with 32 Graviton cores, 128 GB RAM, and a 1900 GB NVMe SSD, delivering top-tier database and web serving performance.
meta_description: Amazon Web Services m9gd.8xlarge is an ARM64 instance with 32 Graviton cores, 128 GB RAM, and a 1900 GB NVMe SSD for database and web workloads.
tagline: An ARM64 general-purpose instance combining dedicated Graviton cores with high-speed local NVMe storage for database and web serving workloads.
bullet_points: 32 dedicated ARM64 AWS Graviton Neoverse-V3 cores running at 2.8 GHz.
128.0 GB of system memory and a bundled 1900 GB NVMe SSD instance store.
Top-tier performance in database operations, memory benchmarks, and web serving.
Average single-core CPU performance and LLM inference speeds.
17 Gbps baseline network bandwidth managed by the Nitro hypervisor.
Suited for database hosting, caching layers, and general-purpose enterprise applications.
